summary: Czech-born British playwright known for Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead, Arcadia, Travesties, Leopoldstadt, and The Real Thing. Also a prolific screenwriter (Shakespeare in Love, Brazil).

works.name: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead
works.description: Follows two minor Hamlet characters. Opens with a coin landing on heads repeatedly (92 times). The Player and his troupe perform The Murder of Gonzago. The title characters discuss death as 'the absence of presence.' One stabs The Player with a retractable knife; all the Tragedians clap. Characters hide in barrels on a ship in Act 3. Tim Roth and Gary Oldman star in the film adaptation playing a tennis game of questions.

clues.clue: Coin lands on heads repeatedly — 92 times in a row
examples: an event with a 50% probability occurring 92 times in a row

clues.clue: Follows two minor Hamlet characters
examples: used minor Hamlet characters

clues.clue: One character stabs The Player with a retractable knife; Tragedians clap
examples: stabs The Player with a retractable knife

clues.clue: Film with Tim Roth and Gary Oldman — tennis game of questions
examples: Tim Roth and Gary Oldman play a tennis game of questions

works.name: Arcadia
works.description: Set at Sidley Park, alternating between 1809 and the present. Septimus Hodge tutors the 13-year-old Thomasina Coverly, challenging her to prove Fermat's Last Theorem. Thomasina says 'Mountains are not pyramids and trees are not cones' and 'pooh to Hobbes.' She devises an equation describing a rabbit eating its own babies. She dies in a fire on her 17th birthday. Hannah Jarvis discovers the 'hermit of Sidley Park' was Septimus. Lord Byron is mentioned but never appears. A tortoise named Plautus/Lightning is on the table. Ezra Chater is a terrible poet who died of a monkey bite. The play ends with two characters waltzing.

clues.clue: Septimus Hodge tutors Thomasina at Sidley Park; Fermat's Last Theorem

examples: 'Mountains are not pyramids and trees are not cones'

clues.clue: Hannah Jarvis discovers the hermit of Sidley Park was Septimus
examples: Hannah Jarvis discovers that the 'hermit of Sidley Park' was actually Septimus Hodge

clues.clue: Ezra Chater — terrible poet who died of a monkey bite; not killed by Byron
examples: Ezra Chater... died of a monkey bite

clues.clue: Tortoise named Plautus or Lightning
examples: a tortoise named either Plautus or Lightning

clues.clue: Ends with two characters waltzing; contemplating entropy
examples: ends with two characters waltzing

works.name: Travesties
works.description: Set in 1917 Zurich. Elderly Henry Carr reminisces about encounters with James Joyce, Vladimir Lenin, and Tristan Tzara. Twists the plot of The Importance of Being Earnest (Tzara and Carr take roles of Jack and Algernon). Carr's wife reveals Bennett was actually English Consul General. Tzara cuts up Shakespeare's Sonnet 18 and pulls words from a hat to impress Gwendolen. Joyce and Carr's real attempt to stage Earnest is fictionalized.

clues.clue: Set in 1917 Zurich with Joyce, Lenin, and Tzara; twists Importance of Being Earnest
examples: twists the plot of The Importance of Being Earnest to be about Tristan Tzara, Lenin, and James Joyce

clues.clue: Elderly Henry Carr reminisces about his encounters
examples: the elderly protagonist Henry Carr

clues.clue: Tzara cuts up Sonnet 18 and pulls words from a hat
examples: cutting up the words of Shakespeare's Sonnet 18 and pulling them out of a hat

works.name: Leopoldstadt
works.description: Stoppard's 2020 play spanning five acts from 1899 to 1955, depicting the Merz family in Vienna's Jewish quarter. Ludwig Jakobovicz plays cat's cradle with Leo Rosenbaum and Nathan Fischbein. Rosa responds 'Auschwitz' to a list of names at the end. Based on Stoppard's own grandparents, who were Holocaust victims. Leo changes his name to Leo Chamberlain and forgets his Jewish past.

clues.clue: Five acts spanning 1899-1955; Merz family in Vienna's Jewish quarter
examples: depicts the lives of the Merz family in the Jewish quarter of Vienna

clues.clue: Rosa responds 'Auschwitz' to a list of names at the end
examples: Rosa responding 'Auschwitz' to the names

clues.clue: Based on Stoppard's Holocaust-victim grandparents
examples: drew on the stories of his grandparents, who were Holocaust victims

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Czech-born British playwright (born Tomas Straussler). Prolific screenwriter: Shakespeare in Love, Brazil (co-written with Monty Python members, creating 'Form 27B/6'), Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade ('almost every dialogue'), Empire of the Sun, adapted Nabokov's Despair for Fassbinder. Admired Thatcher, unusual among contemporaries. Also wrote The Real Thing (Annie has affair with Billy; Henry writes House of Cards play-within-a-play), The Invention of Love (A. E. Housman reflects on love for Moses Jackson), Jumpers (logical positivist gymnasts), The Real Inspector Hound (critics Moon and Birdboot), Every Good Boy Deserves Favour (Soviet dissident), The Coast of Utopia trilogy, and Dogg's Hamlet/Cahoot's Macbeth.

clues.clue: Screenwriter — Shakespeare in Love, Brazil, Indiana Jones, Empire of the Sun
examples: wrote 'almost every dialogue' of Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade

clues.clue: The Real Thing — Annie, Henry, Billy; House of Cards play-within-a-play
examples: Annie begins an affair with Billy

clues.clue: The Invention of Love — Housman reflects on Moses Jackson
examples: this poet reflects on his lifelong love for Moses Jackson

clues.clue: Admired Thatcher — unusual among contemporaries
examples: this playwright admired Thatcher's policies, causing controversy

comprehensive_summary:
Tom Stoppard (born 1937 as Tomas Straussler) is a Czech-born British playwright and screenwriter known for intellectually playful, structurally inventive drama.

Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead follows two minor Hamlet characters who open the play observing a coin land on heads 92 times consecutively. They encounter The Player and his troupe and discuss death as 'the absence of presence.' The film adaptation stars Tim Roth and Gary Oldman.

Arcadia alternates between 1809 and the present at Sidley Park. Septimus Hodge tutors the precocious Thomasina Coverly, who says 'pooh to Hobbes' and devises equations about entropy. Hannah Jarvis discovers the 'hermit of Sidley Park' was Septimus. Ezra Chater is a terrible poet who died of a monkey bite. A tortoise named Plautus or Lightning sits on the table.

Travesties is set in 1917 Zurich where Henry Carr encounters James Joyce, Vladimir Lenin, and Tristan Tzara, twisting the plot of The Importance of Being Earnest. Leopoldstadt spans 1899-1955 depicting a Viennese Jewish family, ending with Rosa responding 'Auschwitz' to a list of names.

Stoppard is also a prolific screenwriter of Shakespeare in Love, Brazil, Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade, and Empire of the Sun. His other plays include The Real Thing, The Invention of Love, Jumpers, The Real Inspector Hound, and The Coast of Utopia trilogy.

cards.front: Play: opens with a coin landing heads 92 times; follows two minor Hamlet characters who encounter The Player's troupe
cards.back: Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead (Tom Stoppard)

cards.front: Play: Septimus Hodge tutors Thomasina Coverly at Sidley Park; she says 'pooh to Hobbes' and devises equations about entropy
Play: Hannah Jarvis discovers the 'hermit of Sidley Park' was Septimus; Ezra Chater died of a monkey bite, not killed by Lord Byron
Play: set in 1917 Zurich with Joyce, Lenin, and Tzara; twists The Importance of Being Earnest; Henry Carr reminisces
Play: five acts from 1899-1955 depicting Vienna's Merz family; ends with Rosa responding 'Auschwitz'; based on the author's grandparents
Playwright: screenwriter of Shakespeare in Love, Brazil (co-created 'Form 27B/6'), and 'almost every dialogue' of Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
